text: The ELSS shall capture all data necessary for recording the fishing activities undertaken by a UK fishing vessel.
The ELSS shall output the data as an XML file for transmission to the UK fisheries administrations' ERS system.
The ELSS shall validate data against the UK XML/XSD before transmission from the fishing vessel.
The ELSS shall transmit data at the required times specified in Section 2.5.1.
The ELSS shall process DAT data operations to capture and deliver formatted ELSS data for transmission.
The ELSS shall process DEL delete operations to capture and deliver formatted deletion requests.
The ELSS shall process COR correction operations to capture and deliver formatted correction requests.
The ELSS shall match received acknowledgements with original messages and provide a record of transmission status.
The ELSS shall provide data capture screens for the entry of logbook, transhipment, and landing declaration data.
The ELSS shall use English (UK) localizations for all UK Electronic Logbook features.
The ELSS shall use UTC for all dates and times.
The ELSS shall provide the ability to print ELSS logbook data using an onboard printer.
The ELSS shall protect electronic print files to prevent modification once generated and distributed.
The ELSS shall transmit data as an XML document attached to an email with a standard Subject header.
The ELSS shall encrypt all XML documents attached to emails using PGP.
The ELSS shall generate and transmit data automatically at least on a daily basis not later than 24:00.
The ELSS shall transmit data immediately after the last fishing operation has been completed.
The ELSS shall transmit data immediately on departing port.
The ELSS shall transmit data immediately after a transhipment.
The ELSS shall transmit data immediately on completion of the Landing declaration.
The ELSS shall transmit the entire report containing all corrected items when performing a data correction.
The ELSS shall identify all corrections and deletions within the user interface.
The ELSS shall only permit correction and deletion messages for reports sent during the current trip prior to the End of Fishing report.
The ELSS shall receive and process RET acknowledgement messages from the ERS system.
The ELSS shall retain all logbook reports and corrections until the end of each trip.
The ELSS shall provide a unique username and password for the owner of each vessel.
The ELSS shall record the username in each report completed and transmission made.
The ELSS shall include a unique internal number in each transmission to identify the software instance.
The ELSS shall alert the Master if no acknowledgement is received within a pre-set time limit.
The ELSS shall use the filename syntax [RSSNumber][YYYYMMDD][999999] for Electronic Logbook files.
